prompt: A surreal, emotionally powerful image of human lungs formed entirely from polluted city landscapes. The lungs are shaped using dense buildings, factories, and traffic. Thick black smoke fills the lungs, making them look suffocated and damaged. One side shows slight clean air with soft light and a few trees, while the other side is dark, toxic, and collapsing into ash. Dark minimal background, strong contrast, cinematic lighting, hyper-detailed, realistic texture. No text, no labels, no numbers, no charts, no infographic style. Magazine cover quality, evokes suffocation, danger, and urgency.
dataset_name: Global Burden of Disease – Air Pollution Deaths
insight: Air pollution is silently destroying human lungs and causing millions of deaths.
